title: Deliciously Citrusy Japanese Colby Jack Cheese Nabemono
description: A twist on a traditional Japanese hot pot dish, this recipe combines the flavors of citrus, ginger, and Colby Jack cheese for a deliciously unique meal.
instructions: Combine sake, mirin, soy sauce, sugar, and 4 cups water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add sushi-grade salmon fillets to the saucepan and cook for 2 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Meanwhile, prepare a salt bake by mixing 1 cup kosher salt, 1/4 cup sugar, and 1/4 cup water in a small bowl until well combined.
Shape the mixture into a rectangle and place it in a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 10 minutes, or until the salt mixture is set and crispy.
While the salt bake is baking, melt Colby Jack cheese in a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Assemble the nabemono by placing cooked salmon, Meyer lemon slices, ginger slices, and green onions in a large serving dish.
Pour the hot sake sauce over the top and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Place the salt bake in the center of the serving dish and top with melted Colby Jack cheese.
